title: DSDS Guideline — Scoped Unions
description: Defines scoped guideline unions for each artifact type. Every piece of structured documentation attached to an artifact — best practices, anatomy, API specifications, variant definitions, state descriptions, accessibility specs, purpose, examples, design specifications, principles, scales, motion, content, and interactions — is a guideline object with a `type` discriminator. Each artifact type accepts only the guideline types relevant to it: component guidelines accept component-specific and general types, style guidelines accept style-specific and general types, etc. This scoping ensures that authors cannot attach irrelevant documentation to an artifact (e.g., an API specification on a style). Internal artifact relationships are now expressed through the unified `links` array on each entity rather than through a dedicated guideline type.

generalGuideline.description: A guideline type available on all artifact types. General guidelines cover cross-cutting documentation concerns that apply regardless of whether the artifact is a component, token, style, or pattern.

oneOf.description: Actionable usage rules paired with rationale. Tells the reader what to do (or not do) and why.
Scenario-driven guidance for when to use and when not to use the artifact.
Structured accessibility specifications — keyboard interactions, ARIA attributes, screen reader behavior, focus management, color contrast, and motion considerations.

Structured content guidelines — recommended action labels with definitions, usage context, and cross-references, plus localization and internationalization considerations such as RTL behavior and text expansion.

componentGuideline.description: A guideline type accepted on component artifacts. Includes component-specific types (anatomy, API, variants, states, design specifications) and all general types (best practices, purpose, accessibility, examples, content).

oneOf.description: The visual structure of the component — its named sub-elements (parts), their associated design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.

The code-level interface — configurable properties, emitted events, content slots, CSS custom properties, CSS shadow parts, data attributes, and public methods. For multi-platform components, include one API guideline per platform.

All dimensions of visual or behavioral variation — each axis (size, emphasis, etc.) with its enumerated values, token overrides, previews, and purpose. Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
All interactive states the component can enter — hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, etc. — with triggers, token overrides, and previews.
Measurable visual specifications — token inventory, spacing relationships, dimension constraints, typography settings, and responsive behavior.

styleGuideline.description: A guideline type accepted on style artifacts. Includes style-specific types (principles, scales, motion) and all general types (best practices, purpose, accessibility, examples, content).

oneOf.description: High-level guiding principles that shape decision-making for the visual domain — what the system believes and what constraints it accepts.
An ordered sequence of values forming a visual scale — a type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale, or similar progression. Each step references a design token.
Named easing curves with cubic-bezier functions (aligned with DTCG cubicBezier type), recommended durations, and usage guidance for the motion system.

patternGuideline.description: A guideline type accepted on pattern artifacts. Includes pattern-specific types (interactions) and shared structural types (anatomy, variants, states) for documenting pattern sub-types, visual structure, and states. Also includes all general types (best practices, purpose, accessibility, examples, content).

oneOf.description: The interaction flow of the pattern — an ordered sequence of steps describing triggers, system responses, and component behavior.

The visual structure of the pattern — its named sub-elements (e.g., image, title, body, primary action, secondary CTA for an empty state pattern), their associated design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.

Dimensions of variation within the pattern — sub-types, modes, or configurations (e.g., an empty state pattern with 'no-data', 'user-action', and 'error-management' variants). Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
States the pattern can enter — populated, empty, loading, error, etc. — with triggers, visual changes, and optional previews.

tokenGuideline.description: A guideline type accepted on token and token group artifacts. Tokens use only general guideline types — they do not have component-specific, style-specific, or pattern-specific documentation concerns.
